title: saponaria prostrata var. calvertii
intro: Saponaria prostrata var. calvertii, also known as rock pink or creeping soapwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Saponaria prostrata var. calvertii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and roots in water or a solvent.
